title: Aureline protected user-journey trace record
description: Boundary schema for one protected user-journey trace record the journey harness (tools/journey_harness) emits for startup, shell-open, placeholder file-open / edit / save, and restore-adjacent flows. The record layers over the spike-timing trace (schemas/traces/spike_timing.schema.json): every journey checkpoint carries a protected-path token from the same vocabulary as the spike trace so two surfaces that measure the same bucket cannot rename each other's concepts. The record pins every journey to a single fixture id (from fixtures/benchmarks/corpus_manifest.yaml), a single minimum build-identity record, reserved hardware and environment refs so later lanes wire in without a schema version bump, a degraded and fallback posture per journey, and provisional segment ids that a later trace-taxonomy normalization can promote without renaming fields. Raw log bodies, raw trace bytes, raw file paths outside the repository, and raw signing material never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque ids, typed tokens, integer ticks, and human-readable notes only. Adding a new journey_class, checkpoint_class, segment_class, degraded_posture, or fallback_posture value is additive-minor and requires a schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

journey_trace_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this journey-trace export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

schema_token.description: Frozen schema discriminator. Every journey-trace record carries schema = aureline.journey_trace.v1 so consumers that multiplex several trace families on one parser can route by field.

record_kind.description: Frozen record-kind discriminator. Paired with schema for parsers that route by either field.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable id. Used for trace_id, journey_id, fixture_ref, checkpoint_id, segment_id, hardware_definition_ref, environment_ref, evidence_refs, requirement_refs, and linked_spike_trace_refs. No raw URLs, raw file paths outside the repository, raw logs, or raw signing material appear here.

manifest_id.description: Stable id of the protected benchmark corpus manifest the fixture_ref resolves against. Currently aureline.protected_benchmark_corpus.

manifest_revision.description: Manifest revision the fixture_ref was resolved against. Pinned so later comparability work can tell two runs apart when the manifest rev-bumps.

corpus_manifest_ref_record.description: Pins the corpus manifest revision the fixture_ref was read against so later comparability and trend work never guesses which corpus revision a journey was run under.

crate_name.description: Cargo crate name of the emitter that produced the journey. In the M0 journey harness this is usually aureline-shell-spike (for journeys sourced from the shell spike) or aureline-journey-harness (for journeys synthesised directly by the harness).

crate_version.description: Crate version string embedded at emit time (CARGO_PKG_VERSION for Rust emitters, the harness pinned version for Python emitters).

rustc_target_triple.description: Compact target triple reported by the emitter (apple-darwin, unknown-linux-gnu, pc-windows-msvc, unknown). The exact-build identity record in schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json is the super-set this field hooks into; the journey harness carries only the minimum it self-reports. The full exact_build_identity_ref rides on exact_build_identity_ref below when a lane has one.

build_identity_record.description: Minimum build-identity hook carried on every journey trace so later taxonomy work can upgrade to the full exact-build identity record without renaming fields. See docs/build/exact_build_identity_model.md.

backend_class.description: Frozen backend-selection vocabulary. headless / native_window mirror CapabilityManifest.Backend in the shell spike; synthesised is reserved for journeys the harness composes directly from corpus metadata (no shell spike run).

host_os_class.description: Frozen host-OS vocabulary. unknown covers build hosts that do not match the other three; it is admissible so the schema does not reject developer-local builds on niche hosts.

protected_journey_class.description: Closed protected-journey vocabulary. Union of docs/benchmarks/spike_metric_names.md §Protected-path vocabulary and the protected_journeys block of fixtures/benchmarks/corpus_manifest.yaml. Two surfaces that share a protected_journey value measure against the same journey budget; a harness MUST NOT invent a synonym.

journey_class.description: Frozen journey-class vocabulary. One record describes exactly one journey_class; a multi-journey flow is represented by several records linked through linked_spike_trace_refs or linked_journey_trace_refs. open_edit_save covers the placeholder open / edit / save lifecycle in one record because those checkpoints compose against one fixture. restore_adjacent covers the narrow restore flow the M0 harness can exercise; recovery_journal_restore_flow is reserved for later lanes that drive the full recovery-journal replay.

checkpoint_class.description: Closed checkpoint-class vocabulary. journey_start and journey_end bracket every trace. protected_path_event is the default for a checkpoint on the protected-path list (spike hooks or corpus milestones). degraded_transition / fallback_transition mark the explicit entry into the named posture. provisional_segment_boundary is reserved for checkpoints a later taxonomy lane may rename; emitters MAY use it to avoid inventing a new checkpoint class for a single trial segment.

segment_class.description: Closed segment-class vocabulary. Every value except provisional_segment is also a protected_journey_class value; provisional_segment is reserved for spans a later taxonomy lane may rename without a schema version bump.

degraded_posture_class.description: Closed degraded-posture vocabulary. healthy is the default when the journey ran without a visible degradation; the other values mirror the product-visible degraded states the shell exposes (degraded renderer banner, responsive fallback, layout-only or compatible restore after missing target).

fallback_posture_class.description: Closed fallback-posture vocabulary. none is the default; the other values mirror the ADR-0002 fallback / atlas / renderer postures plus the recovery-journal replay path for restore-adjacent journeys.

tick_value.description: Non-negative integer tick. In the harness-seeded fixture scene this is a synthetic monotonic tick (0..N) so committed fixtures stay byte-stable; in a wall-clock run this is nanoseconds since the journey started.

checkpoint_id.description: Stable id for the checkpoint inside the trace. Unique per trace; used as from_checkpoint_id / to_checkpoint_id on segments.

spike_hook_ref.description: Optional name of the spike-timing hook (schemas/traces/spike_timing.schema.json#hook_name) that produced this checkpoint. Null when the harness synthesised the checkpoint from corpus metadata rather than reading a spike trace.

note.description: Free-form human-readable label carried over from the harness. Not structured.

checkpoint_record.description: One journey checkpoint. journey_start and journey_end checkpoints MUST be present; intermediate checkpoints are journey-specific.

segment_id.description: Stable id for the segment inside the trace. Provisional in M0 — the naming convention is `seg.<journey_class>.<segment_class>` so later taxonomy work can promote without renaming.

from_checkpoint_id.description: checkpoint_id of the segment's start. MUST resolve to a checkpoint in the same trace's checkpoints[] array.

to_checkpoint_id.description: checkpoint_id of the segment's end. MUST resolve to a checkpoint in the same trace's checkpoints[] array whose tick is greater than or equal to the from_checkpoint_id's tick.

degraded_reason.description: Free-form human-readable reason the segment was tagged degraded. Null when the segment ran cleanly. Not structured; the structured posture is on the trace-level degraded_posture and on fallback_posture.

fallback_reason.description: Free-form human-readable reason the segment was tagged fallback. Null when the segment ran cleanly.

segment_record.description: One journey segment. A segment is a span between two checkpoints; duration_ticks is the tick delta between them. The harness may emit overlapping segments (input-to-paint over startup, for example); consumers MUST NOT assume segments partition the tick axis.

counters_record.description: Aggregate counters for one journey trace. degraded_segment_count counts segments whose degraded_reason is non-null; fallback_segment_count counts segments whose fallback_reason is non-null. linked_spike_trace_count is the length of linked_spike_trace_refs.

protected_journeys.description: Non-empty ordered list of protected_journey_class values the trace exercises. At least one entry MUST match the fixture_ref's protected_journeys[] block in the corpus manifest; the harness is responsible for the intersection check.

hardware_definition_ref.description: Optional hardware-definition ref (see schemas/benchmarks/run_result.schema.json#hardware_definition_record). Null in a self-capture or developer-local run; set when a reference capture resolves against a council-approved baseline.

environment_ref.description: Optional environment ref. Reserved for later lanes that distinguish power / thermal / display / input environments; null in M0.

exact_build_identity_ref.description: Optional opaque ref into the full exact-build identity record (schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json). Null when the lane has not yet wired one; the minimum build-identity record on build is always present.

checkpoints.description: Ordered checkpoints for the journey. The first checkpoint MUST carry checkpoint_class = journey_start; the last MUST carry journey_end. checkpoint_ids MUST be unique across the array.

segments.description: Ordered segments for the journey. Every from_checkpoint_id and to_checkpoint_id MUST resolve to a checkpoint in checkpoints. segment_ids MUST be unique across the array.

linked_spike_trace_refs.description: Opaque refs into spike-timing trace records that materialise this journey. The harness may cite one aggregate (full_scene) ref or a list of per-label refs; consumers MUST NOT assume one-to-one checkpoint / spike-mark pairing.

evidence_refs.description: Opaque refs into evidence-consumer channels (benchmark_lab.*, support_bundle.*, release_evidence.*, boundary_truth.*). Reserved slot so later release-evidence and support-export lanes can attach evidence without reshaping the record.

requirement_refs.description: Opaque refs into PRD / ADR / TAD requirements this journey covers. Reserved slot so later requirement-linkage work can compose against journey traces without a schema version bump.

journey_trace_record.description: One journey-trace record. The harness emits one record per journey_class per fixture; a flow that exercises several journeys emits several records keyed on the same fixture_ref with distinct journey_ids.
